purpose: Replace Level 4 Step D's naive σ8 scaling with proper growth-equation calculation

step_E_correction.description: Recompute σ8 properly for each Step C fit
step_E_correction.delta: Step D overestimated S8 by ~0.16, equivalent to ~8σ shift

step_F_fR_Om_fixed.description: f(R) refit with Ωm=0.315 FIXED (Planck prior)
step_F_fR_Om_fixed.result: Optimizer hit H0=60 boundary (cannot reconcile H0=73 with Ωm=0.315 in late-time MG)
step_F_fR_Om_fixed.interpretation: f(R) needs LOW Ωm to fit SH0ES H0; cannot do both with Ωm=0.315

step_G_EDE_proper.result: Optimizer failed (boundary issues in our parametrization); needs class_ede or AxiCLASS

verdict: Level 4's 'f(R) wins on H0+S8' headline was an artifact of using a naive sigma8 scaling. When sigma8 is computed properly, no late-time modification with low Omega_m simultaneously satisfies H0=73 and S8~0.76. EDE remains the leading candidate, but with a moderate (~2-3 sigma) S8 pull — not the +6sigma I'd reported. The simultaneous H0+S8 problem requires full Boltzmann code work (CLASS_EDE, MGCAMB) outside this sandbox.
next_steps_outside_sandbox: Install class_ede / AxiCLASS on local machine (gcc/make required)
Run cobaya MCMC with full CMB + Pantheon+ + BAO + KiDS + DES
Test interacting DE (Q-coupling) and decaying DM models
If a candidate survives MCMC: arxiv preprint draft
